bullet_points: Load the three datasets from the provided paths.
Convert all series to a common monthly frequency (1987-01 to 2024-01).
Aggregate weekly mortgage rates to monthly arithmetic means.
Perform seasonal decomposition on all series to identify and remove residual seasonality.
Produce a multi-panel diagnostic plot showing raw and seasonally-adjusted time series for all three variables, saved as a PNG file.
Save the cleaned, harmonized dataset as a single file for subsequent analysis.
Conduct Augmented Dickey-Fuller (ADF) and Phillips-Perron (PP) tests on the series to determine integration order.
Perform the Johansen cointegration test to check for long-run relationships.
Test for structural breaks using Zivot-Andrews tests.
Print all test statistics, critical values, and p-values to the console in a tabular format and save to a summary file.
Generate a plot of the Zivot-Andrews test statistic over time for each series, marking the identified break date and 5% critical value.
Define the VAR model using the log-transformed and differenced series.
Implement the block-exogenous structure for mortgage rates.
Determine optimal lag length using information criteria (AIC, BIC, HQIC) and print the results in a table.
Estimate the VAR model and perform diagnostic checks (Portmanteau, Jarque-Bera).
Print full diagnostic results to the console and save the estimated model parameters.
Apply sign restrictions to the impact matrix to identify Demand, Supply, and Policy shocks.
Use the Rubio-Ramirez et al. (2010) algorithm to draw at least 1000 accepted rotation matrices and print the acceptance rate.
Compute median Impulse Response Functions (IRFs) and 68%/95% credible intervals.
Generate a summary plot with separate panels for each shock and response variable, including shading for credible intervals and a zero reference line.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Structural Identification and Impulse Response Analysis

bullet_points: Perform historical decomposition to attribute fluctuations to specific shocks.
Calculate Forecast Error Variance Decomposition (FEVD) at horizons 1, 6, 12, 24, and 36 months.
Print the FEVD table with percentage contributions for each shock.
Generate a historical decomposition plot displaying shock contributions as stacked/overlaid charts, annotated with key historical episodes (2000s boom, 2008 bust, COVID-19).
Write a complete, self-contained Results section including:
(a) Data preprocessing and stationarity findings with cited test statistics.
(b) VAR specification and diagnostic results.
(c) Structural identification and IRF analysis with quantitative discussion of peak responses and persistence.
(d) Historical decomposition tied to named historical episodes.
(e) FEVD analysis with numerical citations from the printed tables.
Ensure all generated plots are referenced by description and provide a concluding assessment of model robustness and market dynamics.
